title: buildah 1.23.1 as root: error extracting layer "a08e1c4f[..]": cannot mount layer, mount label "" too large 4103 > page size 4096
body:
**Preface**: Even though buildah is _not_ running rootless, this is also fixed by using [fuse-overlayfs](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/2d822034f880e802e6f7021c5e/troubleshooting.md#6-rootless-buildah-build-fails-when-using-overlayfs), here via `export STORAGE_OPTS="overlay.mount_program=/usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs"` It is still present in version `1.24.0-dev / Git Commit: d744ebc4` when building from source (but leaving podman unchanged). **Description** On both Fedora 35 and CentOS 8 `buildah bud --layers -f Dockerfile -t my_img:20220112` run as **root** and on bare metal or in a VM fails with (output rewrapped): ``` ERRO[????] error unmounting /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/a666d42c66a66f26cb3f46bd0414bc51ad540b8eacd8f4301188412e931533b8/merged: invalid argument error committing container for step {Env:[PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin] Command:copy Args:[test.dat /] Flags:[] Attrs:map[] Message:COPY test.dat / Original:COPY test.dat /}: error copying layers and metadata for container "e333fca2ffb549bd61765462f8e35733523a1a8fea9f3af48ab2ef4b02059748": initializing source containers-storage:8b2704fb4bf2e73e21d28e7acadb0828be2b25974360e270cb602049dce9eef5-working-container: error extracting layer "a08e1c4f226de4fd3e6b82044db9c34a504b7bfbb4a6b45ca52f0283474fb9d0": cannot mount layer, mount label "" too large 4103 > page size 4096 ``` I have not seen the `mount label "" too large` error message here. Is there a system limit which could be increased? It is always `STEP 65/75` which fails, even if `COPY ...` is replaced with a `RUN` or a different `COPY` directive. The `ERRO[????]` values are e.g. 0010, 0011, 0074, 1126, do these mean anything?
Version information [..] For CentOS 8 / Fedora 35 **Output of `rpm -q buildah`:** `buildah-1.22.3-2.module_el8.5.0+911+f19012f9.x86_64` / `buildah-1.23.1-1.fc35.x86_64` **Output of `buildah version`:** ``` Version: 1.22.4-dev Go Version: go1.16.7 Image Spec: 1.0.1-dev Runtime Spec: 1.0.2-dev CNI Spec: 0.4.0 libcni Version: v0.8.1 image Version: 5.15.2 Git Commit: Built: Fri Aug 27 22:26:38 2021 OS/Arch: linux/amd64 ``` ``` Version: 1.23.1 Go Version: go1.16.8 Image Spec: 1.0.1-dev Runtime Spec: 1.0.2-dev CNI Spec: 0.4.0 libcni Version: v0.8.1 image Version: 5.16.0 Git Commit: Built: Tue Sep 28 20:26:37 2021 OS/Arch: linux/amd64 BuildPlatform: linux/amd64 ``` The current main branch build on Fedora 35 (removing the local buildah rpm and using the path below instead): ``` $ /local/buildah/src/github.com/containers/buildah/bin/buildah version Version: 1.24.0-dev Go Version: go1.16.12 Image Spec: 1.0.2-dev Runtime Spec: 1.0.2-dev CNI Spec: 1.0.0 libcni Version: v1.0.1 image Version: 5.18.0 Git Commit: d744ebc4 Built: Wed Jan 12 19:31:29 2022 OS/Arch: linux/amd64 BuildPlatform: linux/amd64 ``` **Output of `cat /etc/os-release`:** ``` NAME="CentOS Linux" VERSION="8" ID="centos" ID_LIKE="rhel fedora" VERSION_ID="8" PLATFORM_ID="platform:el8" PRETTY_NAME="CentOS Linux 8" ANSI_COLOR="0;31" CPE_NAME="cpe:/o:centos:centos:8" HOME_URL="https://centos.org/" BUG_REPORT_URL="https://bugs.centos.org/" CENTOS_MANTISBT_PROJECT="CentOS-8" CENTOS_MANTISBT_PROJECT_VERSION="8" ``` ``` NAME="Fedora Linux" VERSION="35 (Thirty Five)" ID=fedora VERSION_ID=35 VERSION_CODENAME="" PLATFORM_ID="platform:f35" PRETTY_NAME="Fedora Linux 35 (Thirty Five)" ANSI_COLOR="0;38;2;60;110;180" LOGO=fedora-logo-icon CPE_NAME="cpe:/o:fedoraproject:fedora:35" HOME_URL="https://fedoraproject.org/" DOCUMENTATION_URL="https://docs.fedoraproject.org/en-US/fedora/f35/system-administrators-guide/" SUPPORT_URL="https://ask.fedoraproject.org/" BUG_REPORT_URL="https://bugzilla.redhat.com/" REDHAT_BUGZILLA_PRODUCT="Fedora" REDHAT_BUGZILLA_PRODUCT_VERSION=35 REDHAT_SUPPORT_PRODUCT="Fedora" REDHAT_SUPPORT_PRODUCT_VERSION=35 PRIVACY_POLICY_URL="https://fedoraproject.org/wiki/Legal:PrivacyPolicy" ``` **Output of `uname -a`:** ``` Linux buildc8 4.18.0-348.7.1.el8_5.x86_64 #1 SMP Wed Dec 22 13:25:12 UTC 2021 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux ``` ``` Linux buildf35 5.15.13-200.fc35.x86_64 #1 SMP Wed Jan 5 16:39:13 UTC 2022 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux ``` **Output of `cat /etc/containers/storage.conf | grep -v '#' | grep -v '^$'`:** ``` [storage] driver = "overlay" runroot = "/run/containers/storage" graphroot = "/var/lib/containers/storage" [storage.options] additionalimagestores = [] [storage.options.overlay] mountopt = "nodev,metacopy=on" [storage.options.thinpool] ``` (identical on CentOS / Fedora)

comments.body:
@totph Thanks for creating the issue

How does your `Dockerfile` looks like i mean how many steps do you have ? I am assuming its a single stage with too many `RUN` statements in that single stage.

I believe each new `RUN` carries `lower` from the previous run as well when using `--layers`.  I think in your case the original options flag `lowerdir` for `overlay` keeps on increasing with new `RUN` and eventually it exceeds `4096` which is really odd.

My suspect is `lowerdir=` is breaching the limit in your case.

Again this is my speculation.

**Could you try switching into multiple `stages` after few `RUN`.**

@giuseppe @nalind WDYT ?

Yes I think that's the issue i was able to reproduce this. Specially `lowerdir` is increased when you are using `--layers`  @totph consider splitting into stages. 

My reproducer for `native overlay`: https://pastebin.com/raw/ra2CwmaG

> Could you try switching into multiple stages after few RUN.

I verified that this fixes my reproducer.

we could try some tricks to reduce the length of `lowerdir` but realistically running overlay with that many layers is expensive at runtime too, since each lookup needs to iterate all that directories.

The limit cannot be increased.  The data argument in the mount syscall uses a memory page.

Thank you for investigating! Though checking the `mount` output after a failure I notice that  the number of `:/..` paths is exactly that of the failed `STEP` (here 65), but the length of the `lowerdir=...` argument of the overlay volume is only ~1800, nowhere near 4096.

When I start a new `Containerfile` with the hash from before the failure, e.g. `FROM 377b19b...` I immediately run into the same problem with the next command (even rebooting to get rid of any leftover mounts). Also, it doesn't seem possible to `buildah commit` this hash to a local image.

Splitting this into multiple stages `FROM centos:8 as base_os_c8` and then `FROM base_os_c8` fails after 50 + 15 = 65 steps again:

`[2/2] STEP 15/25: COPY test.dat /` -> `mount label "" too large`

Could you elaborate on the multiple stage approach?

@totph Thanks for confirming yes but `64` is the number where `native overlay` collapses after stacking layers.

> but the length of the lowerdir=... argument of the overlay volume is only ~1800, nowhere near 4096.

That's because you are checking the mount option of `65`'th run where the error is actually printed but the root of the problem was  `64`th run. If you instrument code by a bit you can actually see the data.

```diff
diff --git a/vendor/github.com/containers/storage/drivers/overlay/overlay.go b/vendor/github.com/containers/storage/drivers/overlay/overlay.go
index 15ba5746..2770310a 100644
--- a/vendor/github.com/containers/storage/drivers/overlay/overlay.go
+++ b/vendor/github.com/containers/storage/drivers/overlay/overlay.go
@@ -1524,6 +1524,8 @@ func (d *Driver) get(id string, disableShifting bool, options graphdriver.MountO
                return "", err
        }
 
+       fmt.Printf("mountData %+v\n", len(mountData));
+       fmt.Printf("mountData %+v\n", mountData);
```

> Splitting this into multiple stages FROM centos:8 as base_os_c8 and then FROM base_os_c8 fails after 50 + 15 = 65 steps again:

This is not what i meant. You are reusing layers here. By multi-stating I meant don't reuse the `stage` just copy data from older stages.

```Dockerfile
FROM centos:8 as base_os_c8
RUN do something something
RUN do something something
# Your 50 steps

FROM centos:8
COPY --from=base_os_c8 /some/path /dest/path
# Your 15 steps
```

@totph Please try this ^

I see, thanks. Adding the prefix `/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/` to `l/D5PHTJVW2Z7QD6CI7GSVV5NUOC` is what doubles the mountData length. A quick workaround is setting `graphroot = "/tmp/s"` in the `/etc/containers/storage.conf`. But then `podman` (v3.4.4) doesn't see it and the data has to be copied to `/var/lib/containers` (or pushed to a registry I presume). The podman overlay mount however does not use the `/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/` prefix. Is that a fusefs vs. kernel overlayfs thing?

Unfortunately symlinks from `/tmp/s` to `/var/..` are automatically resolved, so the storage has to actually reside there. If they are resolved by `buildah`, might that be an opt-in fix? Or if the kernel does not accept `lowerdir=` symlink arguments, then maybe a bind mount (which requires root)? The config mentions an even longer `$HOME/.local/share/containers/storage` path for rootless, that would reduce the number of usable layers even more. Also, hashes might use `a-z` in addition to `0-9A-Z` and encode the same information but reducing the length from 26 to 16.

Here `COPY --from=` does not help because only individual files can be copied. The first action is installing various large packages, so `COPY --from=img /* /` would be required. Using an intermediate file via `buildah save/load` does not work because these commands are not supported.

Is there another way to collapse layers into a single one?

@totph Then two immediate solutions i could think of is

* Try reducing your `RUNs` using `&&` i.e merge multiple `RUN` into one.
```dockerfille
RUN something && something
```

* Split your whole build into two seperate builds and squash the first one.

```dockerfile
FROM centos:8 as base_os_c8
... your 50 steps
```
`buildah build --squash -t firstphase .`

```dockerfile
FROM firstphase
```

`buildah build -t finalbuild .`


Btw this issue is a really good `RFC` for inline `--squash`.

Thank you, splitting the build process into two phases with `--squash` allows us to keep the fine grained single `RUN` and `COPY` lines. And indeed, an inline `SQUASH` would help, the `mount label too large` error message could then suggest that.

I noticed that using `--layers --squash` in combination does not make sense as all the "new layers [are squashed] into a single new layer". To still get caching of intermediate steps first one run without `--squash` seems to be required.

> Thank you, splitting the build process into two phases with `--squash` allows us to keep the fine grained single `RUN` and `COPY` lines. And indeed, an inline `SQUASH` would help, the `mount label too large` error message could then suggest that.
> 

@totph No issues yes this workaround would make things work for now.

> I noticed that using `--layers --squash` in combination does not make sense as all the "new layers [are squashed] into a single new layer". To still get caching of intermediate steps first one run without `--squash` seems to be required.

We do have caching in squash as well it was added here: https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/3674
@totph If your issue is resolved. Could you close the issue and we could still continue the discussion here.
@totph I'm closing this since this looks resolved.

Hello, I bump this issue as I hit the exact same issue with multiple AI/ML Dockerfiles, let me know if I should create another ticket

see there:
- https://github.com/openshift-psap/training_results_v0.7/commit/0eda76f902f536a9e5bb4cb003b4229f4ea3bb7c
- https://github.com/openshift-psap/DeepLearningExamples/commit/713071fab18415db86dd3da9bcc7bed5658a3960 (currently trying to work around it)

the base images are `nvcr.io/nvidia/pytorch:20.06-py3`  and `nvcr.io/nvidia/pytorch:20.06-py3`, (at least 66 blobs the latest)

last time I tried, I had the same issue in OpenShift (`BuildConfig`) and Podman locally ...

would it be a solution to squash the `FROM` image? (I'll try it)
anything we could do to avoid touching the original images ...?

@kpouget Unfortunately since we hit `memory page limit` I think `squash` is the best solution which is present as of now as described here: https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/3708#issuecomment-1015706918 or optimizing your `Container/Dockerfile`

thanks, good to know that squash should work around it :)
... but I don't have enough disk space to do it locally with NVIDIA's image :|

Do you know if BuildConfigs have the ability to do the squashing?
I tested this as a first try
```
---
apiVersion: build.openshift.io/v1
kind: BuildConfig
metadata:
  name: tensorflow-base
spec:
  output:
    to:
      kind: ImageStreamTag
      name: aiml:tensorflow-20.06-tf1-py3
  source:
    dockerfile: |2
      FROM nvcr.io/nvidia/tensorflow:20.06-tf1-py3
      # RUN echo hello
    type: Dockerfile
  strategy:
    dockerStrategy:
      from:
        kind: DockerImage
        name: nvcr.io/nvidia/tensorflow:20.06-tf1-py3
    type: Docker
  triggers:
  - type: ConfigChange
```
but the `RUN echo hello` already triggers the error :/

> Do you know if BuildConfigs have the ability to do the squashing?

[found it](https://docs.openshift.com/container-platform/4.9/cicd/builds/build-strategies.html#builds-strategy-docker-squash-layers_build-strategies), but without luck :(
```
---
apiVersion: build.openshift.io/v1
kind: BuildConfig
metadata:
  name: tensorflow-base
spec:
  output:
    to:
      kind: ImageStreamTag
      name: aiml:tensorflow-20.06-tf1-py3
  source:
    dockerfile: |2
      FROM nvcr.io/nvidia/tensorflow:20.06-tf1-py3
    type: Dockerfile
  strategy:
    dockerStrategy:
      imageOptimizationPolicy: SkipLayers
      from:
        kind: DockerImage
        name: nvcr.io/nvidia/tensorflow:20.06-tf1-py3
    type: Docker
  triggers:
  - type: ConfigChange
```
```
STEP 1/3: FROM nvcr.io/nvidia/tensorflow:20.06-tf1-py3
STEP 2/3: ENV "OPENSHIFT_BUILD_NAME"="tensorflow-base-1" "OPENSHIFT_BUILD_NAMESPACE"="matrix-benchmarking"
STEP 3/3: LABEL "io.openshift.build.name"="tensorflow-base-1" "io.openshift.build.namespace"="matrix-benchmarking"
COMMIT temp.builder.openshift.io/matrix-benchmarking/tensorflow-base-1:ae623069
[...]: error copying layers and metadata for container "7fc97b9ae59548c778c701915520cd594168a91bdb326c1ba49751c8049198a0": initializing source containers-storage:tensorflow-working-container: error creating new image source "containers-storage:tensorflow-working-container":
error extracting layer "8ade4dd914184557465403436058427439a06a5081492ee74736752cc934d6df": 
cannot mount layer, mount label "" too large 4233 > page size 4096
```

Adding `--squash` helped me on RHEL-8.5 bud honestly the experience is terrible :(
how many layers are you using for your image?
Well, I guess more than 70 but it fails on the 37th :D
@filak-sap Looks like your page size is even lower than the issue reported above cause it usually fails on `65`th layer.
